channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Vance and Musk question the authority of the courts as Trump's agenda faces legal pushback
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top Trump administration officials are openly questioning the judiciary's authority to serve as a check on executive power as the new president's sweeping agenda faces growing pushback from the courts.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Randy Moss returns to ESPN set following his cancer treatment
item.description: “Guys it’s been hard, but I got a lot of love and a lot of people believing in me,” Moss said as he returned to ESPN's “Sunday NFL Countdown” before the Super Bowl. “I’m happy to be here.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration orders consumer protection agency to stop work, closes building
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down the agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: They helped US order airstrikes against Taliban. Now Trump's moves have left those Afghans in limbo
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — They helped the U.S. military order airstrikes against Taliban and Islamic State fighters and worked as drivers and translators during America's longest war. They were set to start new lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump accelerates Guantánamo Bay migrant directive: What to know
item.description: Trump administration officials are rapidly moving forward with President Trump’s directive to turn Guantánamo Bay into a facility that could hold up to 30,000 migrants who are being deported from the United States. Since announcing the move Jan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE arrests illegal migrant accused of entering the country 6 times
item.description: Jorge Carvajal Castrejon, 36, an illegal migrant, was arrested Jan. 28 in Houston after allegedly entering the US six times, during an enhanced ICE operation with partners from the FBI and other agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Iowa's farm country, small towns rally around a native son in football's biggest game
item.description: In the farm town where Cooper DeJean drew up football plays for his friends in elementary school, residents are planning a huge party for the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party. The multi-Grammy winner packed Brennan’s on Friday night, delivering a hit-filled set at the h.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 people charged in fatal stabbing of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police say
item.description: CLARKSVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Two people have been charged in the death of a Fort Campbell soldier who was stabbed nearly 70 times last year, police said Saturday. Sofia Rodas, 35, has been charged with first-degree murder and tampering with evidence in the death of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: For years, the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office in suburban Indianapolis has wanted to partner with federal immigration authorities to identify and detain immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally and facing charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A 15-year-old went to a Brooklyn parade. The NYPD wrongly accused him of a mass shooting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Camden Lee was leaving high school football practice in September when he saw the photograph, splashed across the New York Police Department’s social media accounts, that would soon upend his life.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KRON4

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: States absorb big increases in Medicaid for sicker-than-expected enrollees after COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — States are absorbing substantial increases in health care costs for the poor, as they realize that the people remaining on Medicaid rolls after the COVID-19 pandemic are sicker than anticipated — and costlier to care for.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery seed packages return to Texas
item.description: The state’s Department of Agriculture is warning residents not to open the mystery packages and report the items immediately.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A hearing in a New Mexico courtroom devolved into chaos when three people rushed the defendant in a homicide case, setting off a brawl with flying fists and kicks. Courtroom video cameras recorded the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bond set at $20M for man accused in shooting at Ohio warehouse that killed 2 and wounded 4
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A warehouse worker said in a frantic 911 call that he was shot in the neck and then took the gun from a co-worker after he had shot several others. Two people died and four were injured in the Ohio shooting that sent panicked employees fleeing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: Texas authorities arrested a Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by President Trump on Thursday for online solicitation of a minor.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about what's tying up a $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's Supreme Court heard arguments Thursday over insurance issues that are tying up a potential $4 billion settlement over Maui's catastrophic 2023 wildfire, the deadliest in the U.S. in more than a century.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Sotomayor renews her opposition to the court's ruling that ex-presidents have broad immunity
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said her conservative colleagues are risking the court's legitmacy with decisions affording President Donald Trump broad immunity and overturning longstanding precedents on other issues.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: DOGE officials home in on Medicare, Medicaid offices
item.description: It was unclear which systems DOGE had access to and whether any sensitive medical information was part of the efforts.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9
